title: Full email capabilities - its here!
excerpt:
Hallo, everyone! We are starting to roll out monday.com Email communication app! We would love to hear your thoughts! 
You can add it to your account with this link: 

You can see how it works here:

posts.author: Ronk (Ron Kimhi)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hallo, everyone! We are starting to roll out monday.com Email communication app! We would love to hear your thoughts!

You can add it to your account with this link:

auth.monday.com

Welcome to monday.com | a new way of working

monday.com Work OS is an open platform where anyone can create the tools they need to run every aspect of their work.

You can see how it works here:

emailcommunication1200×630 26.2 KB

posts.author: PolishedGeek (Polished Geek | Platinum Partner)
posts.cookedHtml:
Thrilled to see this officially released, @Ronk !

Question - Are there plans to have the email app handle individual email threads in different items? For example, let’s say I have the same contact on several items. Perhaps we working with them on a few different potential opportunities. I’d like for outgoing emails from the app to be somehow uniquely tracked, so when the contact replies it will go into the right Item. Currently, all email history related to the app goes into every Item with that email as the contact. There’s no individual item level conversation thread.

Is that coming eventually?

posts.author: rbabayoff (Ronen Babayoff @ Easyapps)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey Ron,

Kudos, looks very very promising.

Just tried the bcc feature on a non-existing contact and nothing happened, no item was created.

Cheers,
Ronen Babayoff
Eazyform for monday

posts.author: handsup (Ben Clowney)
posts.cookedHtml: Great news. I assume it isn’t quite ready to function as a support ticket system replacement, but are there plans to add features to make that possible?

posts.author: turboroof (Jorge)
posts.cookedHtml: I tried to send a couple of emails but they never arrived. Is there some configuration needed to get started?

posts.author: nealb (Neal Bailey)
posts.cookedHtml:
I also see that the bcc does not work.
I wrote directly using feedback sometime ago to also ask what the private option means in the settings. It’s connected with the bcc option?

Another note that dark mode for this app needs attention as you cannot read the notes etc. @Ronk

posts.author: doribak (Dori Ribak)
posts.cookedHtml:
Great start but doesn’t work in the flow it should, missing tons of features to make it actually usable.
This feature needs to be part of the UPDATES widget and not a separate one or at least make them work seamlessly together.

  1. When I send an email from that software it doesn’t add automatically to the update tab, no options to add it over there as well. Why there’s absolutely no connection between those widgets “views”? as of now all our “outside” emails at logged in the updates. Logging it in the email widget is useless when it doesn’t show up anywhere else.
  2. Why I can’t reply to an update with this integrated email widget?
  3. I like the template feature but it doesn’t save the title and the button for it is way to hidden, pull this out to the main window.
  4. Capturing outgoing emails that doesn’t work, also this whole BCC model is flawed. If you want people to use it you need to make that it will be automatically added to the BCC field when i reply to emails from the app, not manually copying it.
  5. No choice for font, it sends them in an awful font although written in other…
  6. It does double spaces in email although now showing in the client,
  7. No html or pictures support (for example signature with picture)
  8. No automation connection at all as of now

It’s like you added a widget that doesn’t integrate with anything else on Monday, I don’t get it.

posts.author: ElenaK (Elena Korolkova (Kick Consulting))
posts.cookedHtml:
Woohoo! Great release! I would love to be able to include signatures.

I wasn’t able to use the ‘Bcc capture all’ feature. I think this will be the most use feature of this widget.

Looking forward to hearing updates.

posts.author: ReconRooster (thomas mcnally)
posts.cookedHtml: Also attachments do not work at all.

posts.author: Elifelet (Nathan Bram )
posts.cookedHtml:
Looks great!!
Only Right-to-Left language support is missing, meaning all points or question marks jump to the beginning of the sentence…

posts.author: nielslange (Niels Lange)
posts.cookedHtml:
absolutely brilliant function! 4 suggestions for this:

  • allow multiple templates to be inserted into one mail
  • allow to add a signature
  • please add the columns in form of variables in the templates
  • blanks are added to outgoing emails by accident

Thanks for the great app!

posts.author: stuttters (Jon Stuttfield)
posts.cookedHtml:
This is a big step forwards, and there are a lot of good suggestions here that would make it better.

One I would like to add would make this immediately a lot more useful for anyone trying to use Monday as a business management system.

Currently, the Email function only works if there is an email column. This limits the functionality to a single board, unless you duplicate data (i.e. two boards with copies of the same email address).

What would be more useful is if this could work off a mirrored email column. That would allow us to use it for Ticketing systems, Sales CRMs and anything else that uses a central Contacts board.

Thanks!

posts.author: Sven.H (Sven Hausherr)
posts.cookedHtml:
Would love to try. But your missing a big info: it only works with Gmail and Outlook!
You should mention this somewhere so users don’t go trough the whole process and keep their hopes up just to notice that it doesn’t work
@Ronk Do you have any plans to offer it to more clients than just Gmail/Outlook?

posts.author: jvelasco08 (Justin Velasco)
posts.cookedHtml:
Thank you for this @Ronk !

Been using this now. Hoping that monday.com team will support and improve this app.

posts.author: stuttters (Jon Stuttfield)
posts.cookedHtml: I use Gsuite, but not the Gmail client - should the BCC function work for me? It doesn’t seem to, but a few random messages seem to be getting through (not the ones I’d expect).

posts.author: stuttters (Jon Stuttfield)
posts.cookedHtml:
To answer my own question - no you don’t need to use the Gmail client.

The issue I am seeing appears to be unrelated to email client. Sometimes it works and sometimes it doesn’t - I can’t see a pattern.

posts.author: Knowa (Noah)
posts.cookedHtml: The biggest pain point I am having with the CRMail addon is that we have multiple distinctly different items (ex. individual projects) with the same email contact. For example, Joe is our contact with the concrete company and Joe is the contact in the email column on 4 separate items. When we receive an email from Joe, it automatically adds that email to all 4 items that he is included on. Functionality similar to the Outlook integration that allows us to sort emails based off inclusion of keywords or job names in the subject or body of an email would remedy this issue.

posts.author: stuttters (Jon Stuttfield)
posts.cookedHtml:
Is anyone getting predictable behaviour from this?

Sometimes I get no emails logged when I use the BCC.
Sometimes emails get logged even when I have never previously logged an email with them, or sent one from Monday.

I’m not even sure what the expected behaviour is?

posts.author: tempest (Peter)
posts.cookedHtml:
Just backing this up!
This feature has great potential, but like Sven, I’d love to see an option to send via own SMTP

posts.author: Jax (Jax Carroll)
posts.cookedHtml: We use Gsuite and the CRMail app is working great. But is there a place in Monday to see all new emails? I know I receive a Notification but it doesn’t have any details.

posts.author: doribak (Dori Ribak)
posts.cookedHtml: I’m sending to the BCC from my outlook and it doesn’t do anything

posts.author: hlopezvc (Hernan Lopez)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi,
there is no integration recipe to send emails to a email contact when by example a status change like gmail integrations?

posts.author: shaisolan (Shai Solan)
posts.cookedHtml: There is, add the app as in integration in the Integration Center

posts.author: hlopezvc (Hernan Lopez)
posts.cookedHtml:
hi @shaisolan i cant see a recipe to send an email from the integration center, what im could possibly doing wrong?=

image1267×593 39.4 KB

posts.author: shaisolan (Shai Solan)
posts.cookedHtml:
You are correct, my apologies.
I believe this feature will be available very soon.
Please hold tight.

posts.author: Sven.H (Sven Hausherr)
posts.cookedHtml:
@Ronk do you know what’s the reason that there is no support for using your own SMTP provider of joyce? Why does it have to be Gmail or Outlook?
Are other SMTP providers supported soon or is it going to be a longer waiting time? (like this year or not even this year?)

posts.author: tempest (Peter)
posts.cookedHtml: This would be a highly desired feature indeed.

posts.author: Konstantin (Konstantin)
posts.cookedHtml: @Sven.H My company wants me to send surveys after the order it’s complete. I have to use @companyname.com but we are hosting at rackspace.com. If I’m understanding right, I have to create a “survey@gmail.com” email, for example, and then use the integration that monday.com offers. In that case, the customer will receive an email from the newly created email, right?

posts.author: Sven.H (Sven Hausherr)
posts.cookedHtml: Thats how I understand it right. For us this is not feasible as we want to use our own email domain. I hope Monday will support own smtp servers soon

posts.author: Konstantin (Konstantin)
posts.cookedHtml: I guess you are right. We host with rackspace and I want to set up automatic survey but if a customer receive email from @gmail.com instead of @ourcompany.com it’s just not professional

posts.author: luke (Luke Crossman )
posts.cookedHtml: @Ronk or anyone else involved in the Beta program have there been any advancements since Aug 21?

posts.author: stuttters (Jon Stuttfield)
posts.cookedHtml:
v2 is out and available via Labs.

Any detail to share what this includes @Ronk ?

Thanks,
Jon

posts.author: ncurewitz (Nathan Curewitz)
posts.cookedHtml: Looks to make the email activities worse. When writing an email it shows up bottom right with no way to make it bigger, read more tries to go fullscreen. looks more visual than anything else. attachments are still not great. still at the bottom of the email.

posts.author: AndrewJVR (Andrew Jansen Van Rensburg)
posts.cookedHtml:
Pulling data from columns using the ‘customize’ button is no longer available in the subject line.
Uncertain what changes have been done with the v2 release - we heard that there was a bug with replying to a mail using Outlook integration, in that the mail thread would not be included. It looks like that has been fixed with this update. No changes on the attachments, and the bottom right email box is silly because you cannot access any other part of the screen, so almost pointless? Specifically as I cannot access the “information” view, or anything else for that matter.

posts.author: rkaplan (Richard Kaplan)
posts.cookedHtml:
When I try to install it i get a message saying that it is Restricted.

Is there a way to try the beta app, or has the final app been released?

posts.author: dgkinney (David G. Kinney)
posts.cookedHtml: Yes, it seems like this would be a great app but when I attempt to install it I also get an Access Denied ‘Restricted’ message. What do I need to do to install this? Thanks.

posts.author: AMP (Chris Dagwell)
posts.cookedHtml: Any update on emails that are linked to an item, at the moment all emails from the sender are in all items? It would be ideal to have the emails only relating to a specific item.

posts.author: HelenCC (Helen)
posts.cookedHtml: I just tried to install this app and i got an error message saying “page is restricted” after trying to connect it? Please help!

posts.author: MariosT (Marios Tsiartas)
posts.cookedHtml: Got the same page restricted message. What is going on?

posts.author: edan (Edan)
posts.cookedHtml:
This is working for me but the from name is showing my address instead of my full name.

This would cause people to have multiple threads since I’d sometimes come up as my_name@whatever.com and somethings as My Name - depending on where I’d be replying to the emails from.

I’d also prefer to have my full name as I find it more aesthetic and professional.
